executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Whole-stack certification remains blocked by external owner-loop residue or a failed independent signoff.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not certify green until the external owner loop clears its live residue and independent signoff stays current.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks

findings.title: External owner-loop residue is still live
findings.mechanism: Live health-monitor residue is now localized to the previous marketing-workflow-audit timeout; the timeout budget has been widened, but a fresh clean run has not cleared the residue yet.
findings.root_cause: marketing-workflow-audit timeout residue after timeoutSeconds repair to 5400s
findings.recommended_fix: Let the widened marketing-workflow-audit budget produce one clean rerun, then rerun the owner verification and health monitor before treating the stack as green.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology matches the current runtime state
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows 25 enabled/total-visible jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 10 running jobs, and 0 live last-error jobs.
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology remains coherent while owner-loop work is in flight.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct cron inspection as the source of truth on each watchdog run and avoid conflating persisted disabled history with live runtime topology.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Loop integrity, health-monitor blocker localization, and shared market-intelligence consumption remain coherent after the refresh; remaining blocker classification is externalized correctly.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Let the external owner loop clear the remaining live residue and rerun its owner checks
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Architecture is green, but live external residue should not be mistaken for a fully green stack.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Clears the remaining external red or keeps it localized to the correct owner loop.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the audit against the current live view: 25 enabled jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 10 running jobs, and 0 live last-error jobs.
Removed stale topology mismatch as an architecture-owned blocker so any remaining red stays localized to the external owner loop.
Reconfirmed that code-backed marketing consumers still expose machine-verifiable shared market-intelligence consumption.
Raised marketing-workflow-audit timeout to 5400s after observing a 1148669ms last runtime; live residue remains until one clean rerun clears the old timeout error.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.
independent_verification.previous_artifact_summary: Independent verification found architecture blockers that prevent a healthy verdict.

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 25 total jobs, 25 enabled, 0 disabled, 10 running, and 0 live last-error jobs.
External owner-loop residue is still live even though architecture is green.
Marketing-workflow-audit timeout budget is now 5400s for a last observed 1148669ms runtime; waiting for one clean rerun to clear stale timeout residue.
